passive.name: Passive: Occult Arts - Manipulation
passive.description: When the Main Body's or Puppet's attacks hit an enemy hero (basic attacks have a 30% chance), Yango gains Hero Shards (non-hero units are excluded) and heals for 70 (70 + 1% extra Max HP). The healing skill changes to 'Shard Bag'. Can store up to 150 Hero Shards to synthesize a hero Puppet. Automatically gains 1 Hero Shard every 10–5 seconds. Hero Puppet: Consumes 50 Hero Shards to transform the Puppet into the corresponding enemy hero (same appearance, lasts 10s). The Puppet deals 50% damage and takes 100% extra damage. (For every 15 extra Physical Attack the Main Body has, Puppet damage increases by 1%; for every 80 extra HP, damage taken decreases by 2%).

skill1.name: Skill 1: Occult Arts - Umbra
skill1.description: Casts the Puppet, dealing 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and knocking up enemies in its path for 0.5s. Afterwards, controls the Puppet. The Puppet's basic attacks deal 100% of physical attack as physical damage. When the Puppet is recalled, the Main Body gains a Purify effect, 40% increased Movement Speed, and a 420 (420 + 6% extra Max HP) shield for 1s. If the Puppet is destroyed, the Main Body is revealed, stunned for 2s, and cannot summon the Puppet for 20–16s. The Puppet is automatically destroyed if it remains over 2500 units away from the Main Body for 5s. Normal Puppet: Inherits equipment effects (excluding Resurrection, Rage, Dark Curtain, Mana Shield) and some buff effects. Gold and EXP are shared with the Main Body.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Umbra
forms.description: Casts the Puppet, dealing 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and knocking up enemies in its path for 0.5s. Afterwards, controls the Puppet.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Return
forms.description: Recalls the Puppet, dealing 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path and knocking them up for 0.5s.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Occult Arts - Zhi Chu Luan
skill2.description: Throws 4 hidden weapons, dealing 435 (435 + 165% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in their path. The damage of the second and subsequent weapons hitting the same target is reduced by 50%.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Zhi Chu Luan
forms.description: Throws 4 hidden weapons, dealing 435 (435 + 165% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in their path. The damage of the second and subsequent weapons hitting the same target is reduced by 50%.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Substitution
forms.description: Swaps positions with the Puppet. If the distance between them exceeds 900, the Puppet moves to a location 900 units away from the Main Body, dealing 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path. If cast while Binding, pulls the Main Body to the Puppet's location and transfers control back to the Main Body.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Occult Arts - Shadow Crucifixion
skill3.description: Fires threads and retreats. Each thread that hits the target deals 180 (180 + 90% extra Physical Attack) physical damage. Units hit by 2 threads take additional physical damage equal to 13% of their lost Max HP and are slowed by 30% for 2s.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Shadow Crucifixion
forms.description: Fires threads and retreats. Each thread that hits the target deals 180 (180 + 90% extra Physical Attack) physical damage. Units hit by 2 threads take additional physical damage equal to 13% of their lost Max HP and are slowed by 30% for 2s.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Binding
forms.description: The Puppet deals 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies, slowing them by 250 for 2s. If cast again within 3s, the Puppet will continuously stun enemies after a short delay, dealing 120 (120 + 40% extra Physical Attack) magical damage over time.

skill4.name: Skill 4: Occult Arts - Dispersal
skill4.description: Disappears, becoming immune to CC, and moves to a different location within range after a short delay. Cannot cleanse the stun from the Puppet being destroyed.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Dispersal
forms.description: Disappears, becoming immune to CC, and moves to a different location within range after a short delay. Cannot cleanse the stun from the Puppet being destroyed.

forms.name: Occult Arts - Breakout
forms.description: The Puppet dashes in the target direction, dealing 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path. If cast while Binding, transfers control back to the Main Body.

strengths: Incredibly versatile with 8 different skills between his Main Body and Puppet, offering immense outplay potential.
Can safely secure vision and split-push by mimicking enemies or remotely controlling his Puppet while hiding his Main Body.
High burst damage potential; can easily assassinate the enemy backline by catching them with the Puppet and swapping positions.
weaknesses: Highly complex skill set with a steep learning curve, requiring extensive practice to master the combos.
The Main Body is extremely squishy and will die almost instantly if caught by crowd control.
If the Puppet is destroyed, the Main Body is stunned for a long duration, leaving him completely exposed.
strategy.earlyGame: Since the Main Body is extremely fragile, it's essential to use the Puppet to secure last hits on minions from a safe distance and harass enemies. Until you reach Level 4 and unlock your Ultimate, your kill potential is low, so avoid forced damage trades and prioritize farming EXP and Gold safely.
strategy.midGame: He truly shines from the mid-game once his skills are unlocked. Infiltrate the enemy jungle with the Puppet to secure vision and look for isolated enemy Mages or Marksmen. Wait for their core skills to be on cooldown, then swap the Puppet with the Main Body to quickly close the gap and unleash your burst damage.
strategy.lateGame: He struggles in frontal teamfights, so he needs to play as an Assassin, flanking or attacking the enemy backline from behind. Use the Puppet to pressure enemy carries, and if anyone mispositions, quickly assassinate them and retreat. Use bushes cleverly to ensure your Main Body's position is never exposed.
strategy.teamfight: Join the fight using your Puppet after your allied Tank engages. Restrain (Stun/Slow) the enemy with the Puppet's skills, swap positions with your Main Body, unleash your damage skills like your Ultimate, and strictly follow a 'hit-and-run' tactic to retreat immediately. Managing the Puppet's HP is also critical, as the Main Body will be stunned for a long time if the Puppet is destroyed.
strategy.commonMistakes: The most common mistake is focusing too much on controlling the Puppet, leaving the defenseless Main Body exposed to be killed by enemies. Always hide the Main Body in a safe bush or under a tower. Also, since his combos are complex, it's easy to miscast a skill in a real match and accidentally expose the Main Body in the middle of the enemy formation, making combo practice in training mode essential.

synergy.reason: Provides CC immunity and damage reduction, allowing Yango to safely execute his combos.
Provides a solid frontline with strong CC, creating the space Yango needs to safely set up his Puppet combos from a distance.

counters.reason: Can use stealth to sneak up on Yango's defenseless Main Body and assassinate him in one hit.
Can use her invincibility frames and high mobility to dodge the Puppet's combo, then quickly close the gap and kill Yango's Main Body.

passive.name: Passive: Unyielding Might
passive.description: When taking damage while HP is below 50%, he empowers himself. For 8s, every skill or basic attack that hits an enemy restores 220 (220 + 6% extra Max HP). This effect can only be triggered once every 25 seconds (scales with hero level).

skill1.name: Skill 1: Wind Slash
skill1.description: Swings his greatsword, unleashing a shockwave in the target direction. Deals 420 (420 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range and slows their movement speed by 25% for 2s. If the shockwave hits an enemy, he can cast Wind Slash a second time within 5s in a target direction, dealing 420 (420 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range and knocking them up for 1s.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Gale's Benediction
skill2.description: Deals 300 (300 + 100% extra Physical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies and grants a 500 (500 + 12% extra Max HP) shield for 5s. His next 3 basic attacks are enhanced to deal 150 (150 + 45% extra Physical Attack) true damage. Lasts 8s. Each enhanced basic attack hit reduces the cooldown of Skill 3 by 1s.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Unruly Blade
skill3.description: Throws his chain blade in the target direction. The chain blade stops when it reaches its maximum range or hits an enemy. The chain blade stuns the first enemy hit for 1s, dealing 420 (420 + 10% extra Max HP) physical damage to nearby enemies, and pulls Dun to the blade's location. Upon arrival, deals an additional 420 (420 + 10% extra Max HP) physical damage to nearby enemies. Dun is immune to CC while casting this skill.

hero_027.status_text:
Apprentice Tank 0/100
Tank/Fighter
Defense Tank
Dash/CC
Early Game

strengths: A Fighter/Tank with abundant CC and high durability, making him an excellent frontline initiator.
Provides excellent crowd control and engagement tools, including knock-ups from Skill 1 and long-range ambush potential with his Ultimate.
Possesses immense resilience and comeback potential due to the powerful lifesteal granted by his passive when his HP drops below 50%.
weaknesses: Relies heavily on his passive for healing; vulnerable to burst damage or heavy CC chains before it can be triggered.
Lacks the mobility to escape easily if he dives too deep without his team backing him up.
His recovery is a key strength, making him susceptible to anti-heal items built by the enemy team.
strategy.earlyGame: His base stats in the early game are high, and the knock-up from the second phase of Skill 1 is powerful. Take the initiative by actively trading damage in the Clash Lane. By using basic attacks while gaining a shield with Skill 2, you can deal true damage and gain the upper hand in the lane.
strategy.midGame: Utilize your excellent CC (Crowd Control) and durability to group up with your allied Jungler or Mid Laner and force small skirmishes. The Ultimate (Skill 3) provides both long-range mobility and a stun, making him excellent at punishing poorly positioned enemies and initiating engagements.
strategy.lateGame: Serve as the main tank by holding the frontline and absorbing enemy attacks. When your HP drops below 50%, your passive allows you to heal upon attacking, letting you hold the line to the bitter end. Peeling to protect your allied carries from enemy Assassins is also vital, so switch between offense and defense depending on the situation.
strategy.teamfight: Initiate the fight by diving into the center of the enemy formation or targeting a troublesome backline carry with your Ultimate. Immediately use the knock-up from Skill 1 and the shield from Skill 2 to draw enemy aggro, creating an environment where your allies can deal damage safely. Even in chaotic fights, trust in your passive's healing and stubbornly keep using basic attacks and skills.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is diving too deep into the enemy formation alone with the Ultimate, leaving allies unable to follow up and getting isolated and killed. Always check your allies' positions before diving to ensure they are within follow-up range. Also, since the second phase knock-up requires landing the first phase of Skill 1, precise aiming is necessary.

synergy.reason: Dun establishes the frontline and absorbs enemy skills, allowing Gongsun Li to deal free damage.
Dun's heavy CC locks down enemies, giving Li Bai the time to safely build his stacks and unleash burst damage.

counters.reason: His true damage ignores shields, completely nullifying Dun's durability advantage.
Uses his high mobility and true damage to dodge Dun's CC while unilaterally draining his health.

passive.name: Passive: Frozen Touch
passive.description: Dealing skill damage to an enemy applies a freeze mark for 7s. When 3 stacks are applied, it deals 250 (250 + 30% Magical Attack) magical damage and freezes them for 0.75s. Subsequent freeze effects applied quickly will only last 0.5s.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Ocean of Tears
skill1.description: Summons an impassable water pillar at a target location, dealing 500 (500 + 70% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in range and knocking them up for 1s. The water pillar lasts for 8s, slowing nearby enemies by 10%. If Lady Zhen is within range when the pillar is summoned, she freezes herself, becoming invincible for 3s. If the freeze is manually canceled or ends, the pillar shatters, dealing 150 (150 + 35% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in range and slowing them by 10% for 3s. Enemies at the center of the explosion take double damage.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Flowing Sigh
skill2.description: Fires a water droplet in the target direction. It locks onto enemies and bounces between them up to 5-7 times (increases at Skill Lv.3/5). Each bounce deals 230 (230 + 40% Magical Attack) magical damage. It can also bounce off her water pillar. Once it reaches the max bounce limit, it disappears after 3s.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Destructive Deluge
skill3.description: Releases a wave of water that travels forward, knocking back non-hero enemy units. Upon hitting an enemy hero or reaching its max distance, it forms a water pool, dealing 300 (300 + 65% Magical Attack) magical damage to the first hero hit and slowing their movement speed by 90% for 1s. The pool lasts for 5s, dealing 150 (150 + 32% Magical Attack) magical damage per second to enemies inside and slowing them by 25% for 1.5s.

hero_028.status_text:

Mage
CC Mage

strengths: A CC Mage who can freeze enemies with her passive, boasting an overwhelming impact in team fights.
Her skills bounce and chain between multiple enemies, dealing more damage and CC the more grouped up they are.
Relatively low item dependency and simple mechanics make her easy for beginners to contribute to the team.
weaknesses: Lacks mobility skills, making her very slow and extremely vulnerable to assassin ambushes or positioning mistakes.
Low early game damage, making it hard to dictate the pace of the match until she gets her core items in the mid-game.
strategy.earlyGame: She excels at wave clearing because the water from Skill 2 bounces between minions and enemy heroes. Use minions to hit enemy heroes with Skill 2, stacking her passive freeze to apply pressure. Since she has low mobility, be extremely careful of the enemy Jungler hiding in the river bushes when looking to gank the side lanes.
strategy.midGame: Her strengths shine from the mid-game when teamfights become more frequent. Always stick with your allied Tank or Roamer and continuously cast skills from a safe distance. If you can land Skill 2 bounces in crowded areas and follow up with the knock-up from Skill 1, you can freeze multiple enemies at once and turn the tide in your favor.
strategy.lateGame: The massive AoE damage, slow, and freeze effects of her Ultimate are the keys to winning teamfights. Cast your Ultimate to engulf squishy targets like the enemy Marksman or Mage. However, since you will be a prime target for enemy Assassins, pay close attention to your positioning and never stray out of your allies' protection range.
strategy.teamfight: The ideal combo is to drop your Ultimate on grouped-up enemies and overlap it with Skill 1 and Skill 2 to freeze the entire enemy team for a long duration. Skills are easier to land in narrow jungle paths or around the Overlord and Tyrant pits, so try to fight using the terrain to your advantage.
strategy.commonMistakes: A fatal beginner mistake is getting too close to the enemy and entering the attack range of Assassins or Fighters. Always maintain maximum range during combat. Also, spamming skills without managing your passive freeze stacks (freezes at 3 stacks) means you won't maximize your CC potential, making stack management crucial.

synergy.reason: His knockbacks and stuns hold enemies together, making it much easier for Lady Zhen to hit multiple targets with her skills and ultimate.
His acceleration covers Lady Zhen's lack of mobility, and his lockdown helps her trigger her passive freeze more consistently.

counters.reason: He can use untargetability and high mobility to slip through her CC and instantly burst her down while she's defenseless.
His long-range ambushes and silence can completely delete her before she even has a chance to cast a skill.

passive.name: Passive: Conquest
passive.description: Casting a skill grants 1 stack of enhanced basic attack (up to 5 stacks) and increases attack speed by 35%. The enhanced basic attack unleashes a long-range flurry of slashes, healing for 90 (90 + 1.4% extra Physical Attack) HP (healing is halved against non-hero units).

skill1.name: Skill 1: The King's Blade
skill1.description: Slashes forward, dealing 300 (300 + 90% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path (can be cast up to 3 times). The 3rd slash knocks enemies up for 0.5s. If cast less than 3 times, the cooldown is reduced by 35% for each remaining cast.

skill2.name: Skill 2: The King's Return
skill2.description: Unleashes a powerful wave of sword energy, dealing 320 (320 + 100% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path and slowing them by 25% for 2s. Hitting an enemy hero recovers 200 (200 + 3% extra Max HP) Health (effect halved against non-hero units). Basic attacks reduce the cooldown of The King's Return by 5%. While Sacred Tree Blessing is active, unleashes an additional wave of sword energy.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Sacred Tree Blessing
skill3.description: Calls upon the blessing's power to cleanse himself of CC, dealing 230 (230 + 75% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies. For 8s, increases his Max HP by 450 (450 + 7% extra Max HP) (increases by 20% for every nearby enemy hero) and increases Resistance by 10%. While active, casting The King's Return or enhanced basic attacks unleashes an extra wave of sword energy (dealing 50% damage), and he continuously gains enhanced basic attack stacks. Tap the skill again to cancel it early.

Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter
Berserker Fighter
Lifesteal
Balanced

strengths: Extremely strong in 1v1s and chaotic brawls due to his Ultimate's massive lifesteal and self-buffs.
Possesses multiple chainable dashes, allowing for flexible engages, disengages, and chasing.
Combines CC cleanse, CC immunity, and healing to endure enemy attacks and hold the frontline.
weaknesses: Needs to stick to targets to land basic attacks, making him weak to heavy CC or kiting that restricts his movement.
Struggles to exert his strengths against burst-heavy team comps or enemies who build anti-heal items.
strategy.earlyGame: He excels at short trades thanks to the three consecutive dash attacks and knock-up from Skill 1. By utilizing the attack speed boost from his passive, you can weave basic attacks between skills to maximize damage. He lacks sustain until Level 4, so farm solidly while conserving HP.
strategy.midGame: Once you unlock your Ultimate, you heal HP with every attack, making him unmatched in 1v1 duels. After taking down the Clash Lane tower, split-pushing to draw enemy attention or invading the enemy jungle to steal resources are effective strategies to bring an advantage to the whole team.
strategy.lateGame: In teamfights, charging straight from the front will get you focused and killed before you can even heal. You must play like an Assassin, flanking from the side or rear to ambush the enemy backline (Mages and Marksmen) in sync with your team's engage. Save the CC cleanse effect of your Ultimate and cast it the moment you get hit by fatal CC to counterattack.
strategy.teamfight: Close the gap with Skill 1 and stick to the enemy carry. The basic approach is to pop your Ultimate when your HP drops or when hit by CC, healing massively while wreaking havoc. The healing during his Ultimate is astonishing, so you will often need to confidently keep attacking rather than running away.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common mistake is casting the Ultimate too early and wasting its healing effect, or casting it too late and dying before it triggers. It's best used when your HP drops to around half or the moment you get hit by hard CC. Also, failing to weave basic attacks between Skill 1 casts results in insufficient damage and missing passive stacks, so practice the rhythm.

synergy.reason: His powerful suppression completely locks down a target, guaranteeing Fatih can close the gap, heal, and finish them off.
Uses stealth and his pull to group enemies together, setting up a perfect situation for Fatih to hit multiple targets and gain massive healing.

counters.reason: His true damage and wide attack range allow him to easily overpower Fatih in a brawl before Fatih can heal back up.
Her heavy CC and heavy sword burst damage can silence and completely obliterate Fatih before he can cast his healing skills.

passive.name: Passive: Mounting Rage
passive.description: All skills can cleanse CC. Successfully cleansing CC grants a stack of Rage, increasing his Physical Attack and Attack Range for 6s. Stacks up to 6 times. Rage: Each stack grants 15-29 Physical Attack and 35 Attack Range. Cleansing Hard CC grants 2 stacks, while cleansing Soft CC grants 1 stack.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Holding On To Sanity
skill1.description: Increases Movement Speed by 60% for 3s (decays over time) and enhances his next basic attack. The enhanced basic attack gains the same range as a max-stacked passive and causes him to dash toward the target (even if outside normal attack range), dealing 220 (220 + 50% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Outburst
skill2.description: Stomps the ground, dealing 350 (350 + 110% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range and slowing them by 25% for 2s. If Skill 2 hits an enemy, empowers himself for 3s. While empowered, Attack Speed is increased by 200%, basic attack Lifesteal is increased by 20%, and every basic attack hit restores 30 HP for 3s. If it misses, the Attack Speed, Lifesteal, and extra healing effects are halved.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Leap of Fury
skill3.description: Performs a leaping slash at the target. Hitting an enemy hero grants 1 stack of Rage, deals 320 (320 + 90% extra Physical Attack) true damage, and slows them by 30% for 3s. Hitting an enemy empowers him for 5s: every instance of damage he deals inflicts additional true damage equal to 2% of the target's Max HP.

strengths: His powerful passive allows him to cleanse CC with any skill, letting him ignore disruptions and force his way to targets.
Boasts overwhelming damage output through his Ultimate's true damage combined with the attack speed and lifesteal from Skill 2.
Incredible scaling into the late game once fully equipped, capable of melting even the tankiest enemies in seconds.
weaknesses: Easily neutralized by long-range poke and kiting comps with heavy slows, making it very difficult for him to close the gap.
Extremely reliant on his Ultimate; if it misses or is wasted, his damage output plummets.
strategy.earlyGame: He is relatively weak in the early game because he hasn't accumulated passive stacks (which increase his attack power upon kills or assists). If jungling, aim to reach Level 4 quickly. If in the Clash Lane, avoid forced fights and clear minions under the tower. Safely securing assists during allied ganks is the fastest way to scale.
strategy.midGame: Unlocking his Ultimate grants him true damage and a powerful slow, drastically spiking his kill potential. Actively hunt isolated enemies using the combo: close the gap (or cleanse CC) with Skill 1 → ambush with Ultimate → boost attack speed and lifesteal with Skill 2 to win the duel, steadily racking up kills and passive stacks.
strategy.lateGame: Once he reaches max passive stacks (20 stacks), he gains an incredible amount of physical attack. A full-build Dian Wei boasts enough firepower to melt even Tanks in seconds. However, due to his low mobility, he shouldn't charge head-on in teamfights. Ideally, wait until the enemy's CC skills are exhausted before joining the fray to clean up the remaining enemies.
strategy.teamfight: It is essential to hide in a bush and ambush the enemy backline, or follow up from the rear after an allied engage. Dive the enemy carry with your Ultimate, and instantly crush them using the explosive attack speed and lifesteal from Skill 2. If hit by CC, stay calm, use Skill 1 to cleanse it, and decide whether to pursue or retreat.
strategy.commonMistakes: A typical beginner mistake is wasting Skill 1's CC cleanse just for the movement speed, leaving him unable to cleanse a crucial stun and getting killed. If the enemy has heavy CC, playstyle demands saving Skill 1 exclusively for cleansing. Also, recklessly challenging 1v1s in the weak early game is a major mistake.

synergy.reason: His acceleration and healing support help Dian Wei close the gap and force his massive damage onto the enemy.
His team-wide CC cleanse gives Dian Wei the perfect environment to rampage continuously without worrying about being stopped.

counters.reason: Her high mobility and stat-draining tether completely deny Dian Wei's approach, allowing her to easily kite and whittle him down.
His Ultimate is a powerful suppression that cannot be cleansed by Dian Wei's passive, instantly shutting down his charge.

passive.name: Passive: Niten Ichiryuu
passive.description:
Casting a skill grants 1 stack of Vigor (up to 2 stacks). Based on the current number of stacks, his next basic attack is enhanced differently and consumes 1 Vigor. Enhanced basic attacks are not affected by attack speed, and hitting an enemy reduces the cooldowns of Skill 1 and Skill 2 by 1s.
1 Stack of Vigor:
If the target's HP is above 50%, the next basic attack becomes a dual-sword slash, dealing an additional 120 (120 + 40% extra Physical Attack) physical damage.
If the target's HP is below 50%, the next basic attack becomes a vertical slash, dealing 45 (45 + 15% extra Physical Attack) plus 6% of the target's lost Max HP as physical damage.
2 Stacks of Vigor:
Dashes towards the target, dealing 187 (20 + 100% Physical Attack) physical damage and slowing them by 25% for 1s.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Illuminating Slash
skill1.description: Unleashes sword energy, shooting down enemy projectiles in its path. Deals 390 (390 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path and slows their movement speed by 25% for 1s. Miyamoto Musashi gains Super Armor (CC immunity) while casting this skill.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Extreme Speed
skill2.description: Dashes in the target direction, dealing 210 (210 + 75% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path. If he hits an enemy, he gains a 330 (330 + 6% extra Max HP) shield and reduces the cooldown of this skill by 50%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Duel to the Death
skill3.description:
Locks onto an enemy hero and dashes to their location, dealing 400 (400 + 130% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemy heroes in range and knocking them up for 1s. The target is then marked for a duel for 5s. While the duel lasts, any healing effects the target receives are delayed until the duel ends.
During the dash, Musashi gains Super Armor and 50% damage reduction.

hero_031.status_text:
Apprentice Fighter 0/100
Fighter/Assassin
Assassin Fighter
Dash/Kill
Balanced
Difficulty: Hard

strengths: His ultimate is a targeted, unavoidable lockdown, ensuring he can eliminate key enemy targets.
Skill 1 can shoot down and negate enemy projectiles, completely neutralizing certain enemy mages or marksmen.
His kit has built-in anti-heal through his ultimate's 'Imperil' effect, making him a hard counter to sustain-heavy heroes.
weaknesses: Relatively squishy for a fighter; if he gets caught by CC at the wrong time, he will be burst down quickly.
Due to the nature of his ultimate, he dives deep into the enemy team and can easily be isolated and killed without allied support.
strategy.earlyGame: By managing her passive 'Momentum' stacks, her basic attacks transform into powerful effects (dash, heal, execute). Her early jungle clear speed is average, but once she unlocks her Ultimate at Level 4, she becomes a potent ganker. Utilize the targeted knock-up and anti-heal from her Ultimate to gank lanes where you can secure a kill.
strategy.midGame: She has a huge advantage against Mages and Marksmen since she can neutralize enemy projectiles with Skill 1. In skirmishes, use Skill 1 to erase key enemy skills, and chase them down with the shield and dash from Skill 2. If an enemy carry is isolated, finish them off with your Ultimate to secure objectives.
strategy.lateGame: In teamfights, positioning after diving in with your Ultimate is crucial. Recklessly charging in will draw focused fire, so wait for your allied Tank to absorb some skills, then cast your Ultimate on the enemy's most dangerous damage dealer. Destroy the enemy backline with sudden burst damage and sustained combat power from her 'Momentum'.
strategy.teamfight: Dive in by targeting the enemy carry with your Ultimate, knocking them airborne and applying anti-heal. Upon landing, quickly finish them off by alternating 'Momentum' empowered basic attacks and skills. The ideal scenario is negating enemy counterattacks (especially strong projectiles) with well-timed Skill 1 wind blades, going on a rampage while mitigating damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: Using another skill without weaving a basic attack and wasting the passive empowered basic attack ('Momentum' stack) is a huge loss. You must drill the 'Skill → Empowered Basic Attack → Skill' rhythm into your muscle memory. Furthermore, treating Skill 1 purely as a damage skill instead of using it to negate projectiles is a waste of its potential.

synergy.reason: Can group enemies up, allowing Miyamoto Musashi to hit multiple targets with his AoE skills and deal massive burst damage.
Provides shields and powerful engage tools, greatly increasing Musashi's survivability after he dives into the backline.

counters.reason: Can use her invincibility frames to easily dodge Musashi's skills and counterattack effectively.
His targeted suppression ultimate can instantly lock down Musashi the moment he dives in, completely neutralizing him.

passive.name: Passive: Way of the Sword
passive.description: Hitting non-tower/non-crystal units with basic attacks grants 1 stack of Sword Energy. Lasts 3s. Hitting enemies continuously with basic attacks resets the duration. Max 4 stacks. At 4 stacks, enters the enhanced 'Way of the Sword' state. While enhanced, increases Physical Attack by 30 and unlocks Skill 3.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Stupor Slash
skill1.description:
Dashes in the target direction twice, dealing 350 (350 + 110% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path, stunning them for 0.5s, and granting 1 stack of Sword Energy.
Casting a third time returns him to his original starting position. The interval between each cast must be within 5s.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Stroke of Genius
skill2.description:
Unleashes a sword formation at his current location, dealing 200 (200 + 65% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to nearby enemies. Enemies who touch the edge of the formation take 325 (325 + 115% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and have their movement speed heavily reduced for 1s. It also reduces their Physical Defense by 150 for 3s.
While casting, Li Bai is untargetable. Cleanses remaining debuffs after casting.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Flickering Flurry
skill3.description:
Transforms into phantom sword energy, slashing the target area 5 times, dealing 240 (240 + 60% extra Physical Attack) physical damage per slash. If it hits multiple enemy heroes, the damage is reduced by 15% for each additional hero hit, up to a maximum reduction of 30%.
Cleanses all debuffs upon cast. Li Bai is untargetable during the skill.

hero_032.status_text:
Apprentice Assassin 0/100
Assassin
Roam Assassin
Dash/Kill
Late Game
Difficulty: Very Hard

strengths: Overwhelming mobility with multiple dashes and incredible outplay potential due to his untargetability (invincibility) states.
Skill 2 shreds enemy armor, allowing his Ultimate's AoE damage to easily wipe out squishy targets.
If he gains an early lead, his snowball potential is virtually unmatched.
weaknesses: His Ultimate is locked behind his passive; if he cannot stack basic attacks safely, he cannot fight effectively.
Extremely fragile. If he is hit by hard CC before he can enter his untargetable state, he will die instantly.
strategy.earlyGame: His early jungle clear is relatively slow and he is vulnerable to enemy jungle invades, so aim for Level 4 safely with the help of your allies. His Ultimate requires 4 basic attacks to unlock, so when ganking, you need to creatively build stacks on jungle monsters or minions before heading to the lane.
strategy.midGame: His signature hit-and-run tactic is perfected here: using the double dash and return from Skill 1, along with the untargetability and defense reduction of Skill 2, to deal damage gracefully and return unscathed. Build stacks on jungle monsters, dive the enemy with Skill 1, unleash Skill 2 and Ultimate, and return to your original position to continuously poke down the enemy's HP.
strategy.lateGame: Li Bai's hit-and-run is overwhelmingly powerful during tower sieges or objective standoffs. However, Li Bai himself is extremely squishy, and if enemies ambush his return point (the shadow from Skill 1), he will be easily killed. It's crucial to approach from various angles so enemies can't predict your return position.
strategy.teamfight: Avoid engaging head-on; instead, use nearby minions or monsters to unlock your Ultimate, dash into the enemy backline, deal massive damage, and retreat. Skill 2 and the Ultimate render you untargetable (invincible) during cast, requiring high player skill to use these to dodge fatal enemy damage and CC.
strategy.commonMistakes: A very common mistake is diving into the enemy formation with Skill 1 only to realize the Ultimate isn't unlocked, resulting in a swift death. Always check your stack status before dashing. Another typical beginner mistake is blindly returning to your shadow when enemies are clearly camping it, resulting in a free kill.

synergy.reason: His massive CC and disruption draw all enemy attention and cooldowns, allowing Li Bai to safely enter the fight.
Provides a solid frontline to hold enemies in place while Li Bai stacks his passive and prepares his burst.

counters.reason: His long-range stuns and ultimate lockdown can easily catch Li Bai and ruin his hit-and-run combo.
His suppression ultimate can instantly lock Li Bai down the moment he dashes in, ignoring his untargetability frames and guaranteeing his death.

passive.name: Passive: Chain Reaction
passive.description: Dealing damage to an enemy with basic attacks or skills applies 1 stack of Destruction (applies 5 stacks per hit to enemy minions). At 10 stacks, the target becomes 'Destroyed' for 5s, and Marco Polo recovers 30 energy. When basic attacks or skills hit a 'Destroyed' enemy, they deal an additional 100 (100 + 35% extra Physical Attack) true damage.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Resplendent Revolver
skill1.description:
Fires 5 bullets in the target direction (the number of bullets fired increases based on Attack Speed). While firing, his movement speed is increased by 10%. Each bullet deals 151 (120 + 18% Physical Attack) physical damage to the first enemy hit and restores 10 energy.
Every 3 bullets are treated as 1 basic attack.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Roaming Gun
skill2.description:
Dashes in the target direction.
Passive: When enemy heroes are nearby, Marco Polo's damage is increased by 20% and his movement speed is increased by 15%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Fevered Barrage
skill3.description:
Dashes to the target location and then unleashes a barrage of bullets around him for 3s while slowly moving (firing speed increases with Attack Speed). Each bullet hit deals 213 (170 + 25% Physical Attack) physical damage.
Every 3 bullets fired are treated as 1 basic attack.

hero_033.status_text:
Apprentice Marksman 0/100
Marksman
Agile Marksman
Long-range Poke
Balanced

strengths: His passive deals true damage, allowing him to easily melt high-HP tanks and bruisers.
Possesses multiple mobility skills, giving him incredible kiting potential and survivability for a marksman.
Excels at safely poking down enemies from a distance with Skill 1.
weaknesses: His ultimate is his main source of AoE damage, but it is easily interrupted by any enemy CC.
Lacks any form of crowd control himself, making him unable to peel for himself or setup kills for his team.
Uses energy instead of mana; missing his poke means he will quickly run out of energy and become useless.
strategy.earlyGame: Harassing with Skill 1's continuous shooting is strong, but it can't pierce minions, so positioning is key. Since he relies on Energy rather than Mana, landing Skill 1 to restore Energy is crucial. His early game dueling is weak, so focus purely on farming from a safe distance.
strategy.midGame: Once his attack speed hits certain thresholds (like 130%), the bullet count of his Skill 1 and Ultimate increases, causing a massive spike in damage. As your core items are completed in the mid-game, use your mobility to group up and actively participate in objective fights. Racking up passive stacks by hitting enemies with basic attacks and skills to trigger true damage is the core of his firepower.
strategy.lateGame: Thanks to Skill 2 (blink) and his passive (movement speed boost when enemies are nearby), he possesses extremely high survivability and kiting potential for a Marksman. Dodge enemy Assassin and Fighter engages with Skill 2 while poking them down with Skill 1. When enemy HP is low, look to wipe them out completely with your Ultimate.
strategy.teamfight: Never step forward at the start of a teamfight. Stay behind your Tank, poke the enemy frontline with Skill 1, and build up your passive stacks (defense break/true damage). At the decisive moment when the enemy's major CC is on cooldown and stacks are applied across the enemy team, dive right into the center with your Ultimate (devastating when paired with the Stun summoner spell) to deal massive AoE damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: Despite being a Marksman, diving into the enemy team with your Ultimate at the start of a fight and getting instantly blown up by CC is something to strictly avoid. Marco Polo's Ultimate is an 'execution' skill. Also, missing Skill 1 repeatedly will drain your Energy, leaving you helpless, so improving your aim is a must.

synergy.reason: Provides shields and hard CC protection, heavily boosting Marco Polo's survivability when he dives in with his ultimate.

counters.reason: Her physical damage immunity skill completely negates Marco Polo's sustained damage output in a duel.
His massive shields absorb the poke, and his ultimate's AoE roar can easily interrupt Marco Polo's ultimate.

hero_034.sub_role: Continuous DPS Marksman / Long-Range Poke / Balanced

passive.name: Swiftness
passive.description:
Basic attacks grant 1 stack of Swiftness, each increasing Attack Speed by 3.5%-7% and Movement Speed by 5% (up to 5 stacks).
Every 3rd basic attack fires a random red or blue token.

The target's Physical and Magical Defense is reduced by 50% for 5s, and their vision is revealed. Allies moving toward the target gain 10% Movement Speed.

strengths: Greatly reduces enemy defenses with Ult, crippling even tanky frontlines.
Strong continuous long-range poke and fast tower pushing capabilities.
Synergizes well with bruiser builds, giving him higher survivability than other marksmen.
weaknesses: Lacks a mobility (blink) skill, making him vulnerable to hard engages from assassins.
Highly reliant on positioning; being isolated or mispositioned usually leads to death.
Requires core items to deal significant damage, making it tough to play from behind in gold.
strategy.earlyGame: Thanks to his passive, his attack speed and movement speed increase with every basic attack. By maintaining these stacks during lane phase, he can output very high DPS right from the start. Use your piercing empowered basic attacks through minions to safely poke the enemy Marksman and take control of the lane.
strategy.midGame: Because Skill 2 offers a CC cleanse and brief invincibility, he has far better self-peel than other Marksmen. Even if ganked by the enemy Jungler, he can cleanse slows or stuns with Skill 2 and survive. He excels at taking down towers, so once you destroy your lane's tower, rotate to the Mid Lane to accelerate the game's pace.
strategy.lateGame: Once fully built, his passive combined with items gives him insane attack speed, making his kiting unstoppable. Landing your Ultimate (yellow card) on the enemy frontline (like a Tank) before a teamfight drastically reduces their physical and magical defense (by 50%), allowing you to easily melt even the tankiest enemies.
strategy.teamfight: Always maintain maximum range and continuously land basic attacks on whichever enemy gets closest to you. Use your Ultimate either defensively to stop enemy engages or aggressively on a locked-down target to shred their defense. If an enemy Assassin jumps on you, stay calm, cleanse the CC with Skill 2, reposition with Flash, and counterattack.
strategy.commonMistakes: The most common mistake is wasting Skill 2 as a simple damage tool, leaving you unable to cleanse a crucial CC from an enemy Assassin or Mage when it matters most, resulting in your death. Always save Skill 2 strictly for 'cleansing enemy CC'. Additionally, initiating combat without maintaining passive stacks will lower your damage, so practice stacking them on thin air beforehand.

synergy.reason: Provides massive shields and CC, creating a safe environment for dealing damage.
Her healing and skill cooldown resets greatly improve stability in laning and team fights.

counters.reason: His stealth and high burst damage make it easy to assassinate Di Renjie before he can react.
High mobility allows him to close the gap instantly, making it hard to escape.

passive.name: Mantra: Heart
passive.description:
Casting skills grants a stack of Mantra's Blessing, increasing Physical Defense by 32 and Magical Defense by 32 for 5s (up to 3 stacks).
After using Skill 1 and 3, he gains 1 enhanced basic attack (an additional one if hitting a target). Enhanced basic attacks deal an extra 28 (+30% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and reduce target Movement Speed by 15% for 2s.

skill1.name: Mantra: Formless
skill1.description: Dashes forward with a punch, dealing 150 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in the path and knocking them up for 0.5s. This skill stores 1 charge every 9s (max 2 charges). If an enemy is knocked into a wall by his Ultimate, using this skill within 8s transforms it into a flying kick, dealing additional physical damage equal to 15% of the target's lost HP.

skill2.name: Mantra: Illumination

Each punch deals 65 physical damage (+50% to non-heroes) and physical damage equal to 0.35% of the target's max HP (increases by 0.25% per 100 Extra Physical Attack). Heals 65 (+2.5% Extra HP) HP per hit. During casting, you can change direction with the joystick, automatically firing enhanced basic attacks that apply the same effect as his passive. Healing is halved against non-heroes. Canceling the skill refunds 50%-100% cooldown based on duration cast.

skill3.name: Mantra: Truth
skill3.description: Punches forward, dealing 200 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and knocking enemies back. Enemies knocked into a wall take an additional 200 (+100% Extra Physical Attack) physical damage and are stunned for 1s.

strengths: Possesses game-changing potential with his Ult's massive AoE wall-stun.
Combines tank-like durability with fighter-level damage to pressure the frontline.
Dash skills and strong CC make it easy to isolate and burst down enemy mages or marksmen.
weaknesses: Heavily reliant on team follow-up; struggles to secure kills alone after engaging.
Vulnerable to hard CC before engaging, which can completely shut him down.
High mana consumption makes resource management difficult during laning and extended fights.
strategy.earlyGame: He possesses a passive that heals HP and boosts physical defense with empowered basic attacks after using skills, giving him excellent dueling and lane sustain. Capitalize on the dash and knock-up from Skill 1 to dominate short trades. He lacks kill pressure before Level 4, so refrain from reckless tower dives and focus on farming.
strategy.midGame: Unlocking his Ultimate allows for a brutal combo where he kicks enemies into walls to stun them. He thrives in small skirmishes in tight spaces with many walls, such as the jungle or near towers. Actively perform 'bush kills' by hiding in a bush and ambushing approaching enemies by kicking them into a wall for an instant kill.
strategy.lateGame: Landing your Ultimate on the enemy backline dictates the outcome of teamfights. Use a long-range engage combining Flash and Skill 1 to unexpectedly kick the enemy Marksman or Mage into a wall. However, missing your Ultimate drastically reduces your damage and leaves you isolated deep in enemy territory, making your engagement timing absolutely crucial.
strategy.teamfight: Instead of charging straight in, flank the enemy backline from side bushes. The ultimate setup is using 'Skill 1 → Flash' to close the gap and kicking multiple enemies into a wall with your Ultimate. A successful wall-kick unlocks the second phase of his Ultimate (a flying kick dealing percentage missing HP damage), which, paired with the defense reduction from Skill 2, will instantly obliterate the enemy team.
strategy.commonMistakes: A classic beginner mistake is using the Ultimate in an open area, simply knocking enemies far away without stunning them and accidentally saving them. Always be conscious of 'kicking enemies toward a wall' when using Dharma's Ultimate. Also, sloppy aiming when kicking can knock enemies right out of your allies' skill range, so aim carefully.

synergy.reason: Both heroes possess knock-ups, allowing them to chain powerful CC effectively.
Her reliable stun stops enemies, guaranteeing an easy wall-stun with your Ultimate.

counters.reason: His Ultimate cleanses AoE CC, completely nullifying Dharma's wall-stun.
His Skill 2 removes CC, allowing him to escape and counterattack.

strengths: Incredible survivability due to his massive damage reduction when below 30% HP.
Strong CC skills like the dash-knockup and AoE stun can lock down enemies.
Deals true damage based on max HP through his passive, making him a threat to tanks and towers.
weaknesses: Low mobility outside of Skill 1 makes him susceptible to getting kited by highly mobile heroes.
High skill reliance; missing his engage skills leaves him wide open.
Percent-damage or heavy armor-piercing builds can easily bypass his durability.
strategy.earlyGame: He boasts incredibly high durability thanks to his passive, which significantly reduces damage taken when his HP drops below 30%. The dash and knockback from Skill 1 are perfect for controlling minion waves or disrupting enemies. When playing as a Roamer, your primary role is defensive—preventing enemy jungle invades and protecting your Marksman.
strategy.midGame: Serve as the team's shield on the frontline by utilizing your excellent engage and peel capabilities. When you spot poorly positioned enemies, push them toward your allies with Skill 1, apply the slow and damage reduction from Skill 2, and assist in securing the kill. Conversely, when allies are targeted, push enemies away to buy time for their escape.
strategy.lateGame: His Ultimate deals higher damage the lower his HP gets and grants temporary invincibility (or powerful damage reduction) upon casting. By enduring the enemy's fierce onslaught in teamfights and unleashing your Ultimate at the very last second when your HP is critically low, you can deal devastating AoE burst damage despite being a Tank.
strategy.teamfight: Your top priority is protecting your team's main carry. If an enemy Assassin or Fighter dives in, immediately push them away with Skill 1 and neutralize their firepower with Skill 2. When switching to offense, use the 'Flash + Skill 1' combo for a surprise engage. Once the enemy formation is broken, slam your Ultimate to seal the victory.
strategy.commonMistakes: A frequent 'friendly fire' mistake is using Skill 1 to push a fleeing, low-HP enemy to safety, preventing your allies from finishing them off. Always aim to push enemies 'toward your allies' or 'into a wall'. Also, when his passive damage reduction is on cooldown, he is surprisingly squishy, so be careful not to overextend with misplaced confidence.

synergy.reason: Can push enemies away to keep distance, protecting the immobile marksman.
Xiang Yu leads the charge and restricts enemies, creating an opening for Nakoruru to burst them down safely.

counters.reason: High mobility allows him to bypass defenses, making Xiang Yu's durability less effective.
His defense reduction debuff significantly mitigates Xiang Yu's tankiness.

strengths: Overwhelming burst damage capable of instantly assassinating the enemy backline.

weaknesses: Extremely squishy; if caught by CC, he dies without being able to act.

strategy.earlyGame: Clear the jungle quickly right from the start, and look to snowball by executing a gank the moment you reach Level 4. While managing your Energy carefully, utilize your high mobility to punish any enemies stepping out of position.
strategy.midGame: Once you build some Cooldown Reduction items, you'll be able to cycle your skills continuously. Don't miss any isolated enemy Mages or Marksmen; instantly unleash your burst damage to rack up kills and seize map control.
strategy.lateGame: As enemies start to group up, Sima Yi, being an Assassin, will find it harder to maneuver. Rather than initiating the fight yourself, play the role of the cleanup crew: wait for your team to bait out heavy enemy CC, then dive the enemy backline with your Ultimate to decimate their squishies.
strategy.teamfight: Positioning to flank from the side or behind is vital in teamfights. Dive the enemy carry with your Ultimate, silence them with Skill 2 to prevent counterattacks, instantly delete them with your empowered basic attacks, and immediately retreat using Skill 1 in a classic hit-and-run style.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common beginner mistake is charging head-on into a teamfight, only to get hit by CC and killed without doing anything. Additionally, since his damage relies almost entirely on the empowered basic attacks following his skills, you must remain calm and master the 'Skill → Basic Attack' combo rhythm.

synergy.reason: Great at following up after enemies are poked down, and their high mobility secures multiple kills.
Groups enemies together, making it easier for Sima Yi to land his Ultimate and AoE silence.

counters.reason: Has higher mobility, allowing him to easily escape or counterattack Sima Yi.
His massive shields and CC can completely protect the targeted squishy from burst damage.

strengths: Unrivaled 1v1 dueling strength due to the massive damage reduction from Skill 2.
Extremely fast tower pushing speed thanks to true damage and attack speed from his passive.
Ultimate forcibly locks down enemy carries or highly mobile heroes in a specific area.
weaknesses: Weak early game fighting power; requires careful farming until items are built.
Vulnerable to chain CC; can be bursted down before casting his damage reduction.
Hard to reach the enemy backline in team fights; can be easily kited if not positioned well.
strategy.earlyGame: In the Clash Lane early game, managing your passive gauge is everything. Engaging with a full gauge is incredibly powerful, but you are very weak without it. Attack minions to build up your gauge, and only trade damage when the timing favors you.
strategy.midGame: His unmatched 1v1 dueling prowess makes him ideal for split-pushing deep into the side lanes. Even if the enemy Jungler comes to gank, you can tie one of them down with your Ultimate, endure the assault using the damage reduction from Skill 2, and turn the tables on them.
strategy.lateGame: Once fully built, his tower-taking speed becomes absurd. Push the lane opposite to the objective (Tyrant or Overlord) your team is taking, forcing the enemy into a macro dilemma: 'Do we defend the tower, or contest the objective?' thereby heavily contributing to the team.
strategy.teamfight: You don't need to force yourself into 5v5 teamfights, but if you do group up, your role is to use Flash into your Ultimate to forcibly lock down the enemy carry (Mage or Marksman) and secure a guaranteed kill. Cast Skill 2 right as the enemy unleashes their burst damage to minimize the damage you take.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is wasting Skill 2 (damage reduction and reflection) casually. If you don't time it against the enemy's most damaging skills or basic attacks, you'll just be a sitting duck. Furthermore, prioritizing teamfights over split-pushing prevents you from fully capitalizing on his strengths.

synergy.reason: The combination of split-pushing and recalling allows for a relentless strategy that constantly confuses the enemy.
Fuzi's long lockdown creates the perfect window for Li Bai to safely unleash max burst damage.

counters.reason: Her high mobility and damage reduction gives her the edge in basic attack trades.
Can easily push Fuzi away, making it hard for him to close the gap and stack his passive.

hero_039.status_text:

strengths: Exceptional rotation speed across the map thanks to his passive 'Charge State'.
Powerful knockbacks from his Charge State disrupt enemy formations and isolate targets.
Built-in CC cleanse on Skill 2 allows him to escape interruptions and counterattack.
weaknesses: Damage plummets if movement stops; must keep moving constantly, making him very hard to play.
Weak to chain CC and heavy slows; stopping his charge completely shuts him down.
Heavily relies on team follow-up; disrupting formations is useless if allies can't capitalize.
strategy.earlyGame: Dominate the lane by maintaining your passive's charge state (which boosts your movement speed). Stopping drastically drops your damage, so keep your movement joystick moving constantly. A highly effective strategy is rotating to the Mid Lane to assist with a gank by knocking enemies back from your charge state.
strategy.midGame: Utilize your high mobility to roam the entire map, applying continuous pressure to every lane. Even when enemies are pushed under their own tower, you can secure kills by flanking behind them and pushing them out toward your team (an 'Insec' play).
strategy.lateGame: Once your Cooldown Reduction reaches its cap, you can spam knockbacks almost infinitely while your Ultimate is active. Just before a teamfight, disappear from enemy vision, then charge in from the flank or rear at the perfect moment to completely shatter their formation.
strategy.teamfight: Charging straight in from the front is strictly prohibited. Because any CC (slow or stun) will break your charge state, wait until your team starts the fight before diving in from their blind spots. Push the enemy carries to isolate them, and use the cleanse from Skill 2 to keep rampaging without being stopped.
strategy.commonMistakes: Panicking and lifting your finger off the movement joystick, causing your charge state to drop, is a fatal error. Another common beginner mistake is charging in while the enemy still has their heavy CC skills available, leading to an instant death. You must carefully judge the perfect timing to use the Summoner Spells 'Purify' or 'Sprint'.

weaknesses: Extremely vulnerable to hard CC (especially suppression); she will die instantly if locked down.

strategy.earlyGame: She is weak early on due to high mana consumption, so avoid forced fights and focus purely on clearing minions. Farm safely using Skill 1 and Skill 2. Until your first core item (like Insatiable Tome) is complete, prioritize supporting your Jungler over aggressive plays.
strategy.midGame: Once she gets her spell vamp items and some Cooldown Reduction, she becomes unmatched in small skirmishes. A powerful tactic is to continuously dodge enemy skills with the invincibility frames of Skill 2 while spreading your passive's true damage and healing to slowly whittle down the enemy's health.
strategy.lateGame: A full-build Diaochan boasts terrifying sustain (continuous combat ability) in teamfights. If you can keep dodging focused fire with Skill 2, she has the potential to win 1vX scenarios. However, the enemy will also build anti-heal items, so overconfidence can be deadly.
strategy.teamfight: Never initiate the fight yourself. Wait to confirm that the enemy has blown their major hard CC (especially un-cleansable ones like Suppression) on your Tanks, then enter the fight by laying down your Ultimate's circle. Inside the circle, quickly loop Skill 1 and Skill 2, dealing continuous damage while 'dancing' to dodge enemy attacks.
strategy.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is using Skill 2 purely to dash straight at the enemy. If you don't actively use the brief invincibility frames during the dash to 'dodge enemy damage and CC', you'll just be a sitting duck. Additionally, fighting outside your Ultimate's circle severely drops your damage output, so positional awareness is key.

strengths: Extremely high mobility and continuous dashes using Ultimate resets.

Incredible sustained damage in the mid-to-late game once core items and CDR are acquired.
weaknesses: Extremely vulnerable to CC; easily bursted down if stunned or silenced.
Very high difficulty; failing to reset her Ultimate leaves her isolated in the enemy team.

strategy.earlyGame: She is extremely weak before reaching Level 4 and unlocking her Ultimate, so farm as fast as possible while staying alert for enemy jungle invades. Once at Level 4, apply marks with Skill 1 and Skill 2, and execute continuous dash ganks by resetting your Ultimate to widen the gold gap.
strategy.midGame: When her core items are complete, she exhibits unstoppable dominance in mid-sized teamfights. Look for outplays by using minions or neutral monsters as stepping stones to apply marks, quickly closing the distance to the enemy carry from afar, and bursting them down.
strategy.lateGame: Since enemy damage scales up and CC becomes abundant, a single mistake in resetting your Ultimate can lead to instant death. It is crucial to build defensive items like 'Sage's Sanctuary' (Resurrection Armor) or 'Splendor' (Stasis item), and strictly adhere to hit-and-run tactics to boost your survivability.
strategy.teamfight: Charging head-on will invite focused fire, so wait in the flanking bushes for the right moment during teamfights. The second your allies engage and enemy CC is exhausted, dive in, spread marks across multiple enemies, and wreak havoc on their formation by darting around with your Ultimate.
strategy.commonMistakes: The fatal mistake is casting the Ultimate on an unmarked enemy, causing it to go on cooldown (commonly known as 'breaking the Ult'). Avoid mashing buttons in a panic; you must build the muscle memory in training mode to only swipe your Ultimate after confirming a mark is present.

synergy.reason: Groups enemies together, making it easier for Luna to hit multiple targets and reset her Ultimate.

counters.reason: Hard locks her with point-and-click CC, completely shutting down her mobility.
Reliable CC and burst damage can interrupt her combos and burst her down.

Excels at solo objective taking and split-pushing, applying relentless macro pressure on the enemy.
weaknesses: Extremely squishy; highly susceptible to being bursted down if caught by CC.

strategy.earlyGame: Utilize your incredibly high mobility to clear your jungle as fast as possible and hit Level 4. Afterward, exploit enemy openings to gank or invade the enemy jungle to steal buffs. Sprint across the entire map to widen the gold advantage right from the early game.
strategy.midGame: Avoid direct 5v5 clashes and always opt for guerrilla warfare to annoy the enemy. Push unguarded side lane towers alone, and when multiple enemies come to defend, safely escape using your numerous dash skills. This macro strategy forces enemies out of position, giving your team free rein to take objectives on the opposite side of the map.
strategy.lateGame: As enemy damage peaks in the late game, Han Xin's low durability means he'll melt instantly. Never initiate a teamfight. Instead, act strictly as a 'sweeper' who enters late to execute low-health enemies, or ignore the teamfight entirely to sneakily perform a 'backdoor' and destroy the enemy's base towers or crystal.
strategy.teamfight: Wait in a blind spot (like a bush) and watch until the enemy's CC (crowd control) and high-damage skills are blown. Dive the enemy backline (Marksman or Mage) with 'Skill 2 → Skill 1', endure their counterattacks with the Super Armor (CC immunity) from your Ultimate while dealing burst damage, and then use your remaining skills to escape.

strengths: With its passive stealth (invisibility) ability, it is possible to secure one-sided visibility and approach enemies without them noticing.
Boasting high burst damage from the early stages, it is easy to instantly kill enemy mages and marksmen and snowball them.
If you are in stealth mode, a warning mark will appear on the enemy just by being nearby, allowing you to apply psychological pressure and restrict their actions.

strategy.earlyGame: Take full advantage of the privilege of being able to turn invisible (stealth) from Level 1 to clear the jungle quickly and gank without being noticed. Because your early-game burst damage is incredibly high, your primary job is to target squishy Marksmen and Mages for guaranteed kills to snowball the game.
strategy.midGame: Roam the map relentlessly, hunting for any isolated enemy carries trying to farm. Although a warning mark appears over their heads when you approach in stealth, you can use this to your advantage in a psychological war: 'exert pressure without revealing yourself, forcing enemies under their towers so your team can freely take objectives'.
strategy.lateGame: As enemies build defensive items and group up, Lanling King starts to fall off. Instead of forcing solo kills, transition your playstyle: use your stealth to act as a 'mobile ward' to secure enemy vision, and capitalize on the chaos of teamfights to assassinate low-health stragglers.
strategy.teamfight: Charging in from the front will get you instantly focused and melted. Never reveal yourself before the teamfight truly begins. Wait for your allied Tanks to absorb the enemy's CC, and once the fight turns into a chaotic brawl, flank the enemy backline from the side or rear, unleash your 'Skill 2 → Ultimate → Skill 1' combo, and immediately disengage.
strategy.commonMistakes: Getting too close to enemies carelessly while in stealth, triggering the warning mark and exposing your position, only to get caught in AoE damage or random CC and revealed, is fatal. Also, missing Skill 2 (the dagger) but still blindly diving in with your Ultimate, resulting in insufficient damage and getting killed in retaliation, is a very common blunder.

synergy.reason: This is because it can support the enemy by lowering their defense power and taking their vision, and maximizes Lanlingou's burst damage.
This is because it attracts the enemy's attention in group battles, making it easier for the stealth Prince of Lanling to assassinate marksmen from behind.

counters.reason: Because they can secure their visibility with their skills, they can detect Prince of Lanling's stealth surprise attack in advance and take countermeasures.
He has extremely high durability and CC, and is able to withstand Lanlingo's burst damage and end up being restrained.
